heading: San Francisco Bay restoration grant program
label: 1276a San Francisco Bay restoration grant program

children.heading: Estuary Partnership
children.label: 1 Estuary Partnership
children.text: The term “Estuary Partnership” means the San Francisco Estuary Partnership, designated as the management conference for the San Francisco Bay under .section 1330 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: until the date of the completion of the plan developed by the Director under subsection (d), the comprehensive conservation and management plan approved under for the San Francisco Bay estuary; andsection 1330 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: on and after the date of the completion of the plan developed by the Director under subsection (d), the plan developed by the Director under subsection (d).

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Administrator shall establish in the Environmental Protection Agency a San Francisco Bay Program Office. The Office shall be located at the headquarters of Region 9 of the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.heading: Appointment of Director
children.label: 2 Appointment of Director
children.text: The Administrator shall appoint a Director of the Office, who shall have management experience and technical expertise relating to the San Francisco Bay and be highly qualified to direct the development and implementation of projects, activities, and studies necessary to implement the San Francisco Bay Plan.

children.heading: Delegation of authority; staffing
children.label: 3 Delegation of authority; staffing
children.text: The Administrator shall delegate to the Director such authority and provide such staff as may be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: After providing public notice, the Director shall annually compile a priority list, consistent with the San Francisco Bay Plan, identifying and prioritizing the projects, activities, and studies to be carried out with amounts made available under subsection (e).

children.label: i
children.text: water quality improvement, including the reduction of marine litter;

children.label: ii
children.text: wetland, riverine, and estuary restoration and protection;

children.label: iii
children.text: nearshore and endangered species recovery; and

children.label: iv
children.text: adaptation to climate change.

children.label: i
children.text: the identity of each entity receiving assistance pursuant to subsection (e); and

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the communities to be served.

children.label: C
children.text: The criteria and methods established by the Director for identification of projects, activities, and studies to be included on the annual priority list.

children.label: A
children.text: the Estuary Partnership;

children.label: B
children.text: the State of California and affected local governments in the San Francisco Bay estuary watershed;

children.label: C
children.text: the San Francisco Bay Restoration Authority; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other relevant stakeholder involved with the protection and restoration of the San Francisco Bay estuary that the Director determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , the Director, in conjunction with the Estuary Partnership, shall review and revise the comprehensive conservation and management plan approved under for the San Francisco Bay estuary to develop a plan to guide the projects, activities, and studies of the Office to address the restoration and protection of the San Francisco Bay.December 23, 2022section 1330 of this title

children.heading: Revision of San Francisco Bay Plan
children.label: 2 Revision of San Francisco Bay Plan
children.text: Not less often than once every 5 years after the date of the completion of the plan described in paragraph (1), the Director shall review, and revise as appropriate, the San Francisco Bay Plan.

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: 3 Outreach
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Director shall consult with the Estuary Partnership and Indian tribes and solicit input from other non-Federal stakeholders.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director may provide funding through cooperative agreements, grants, or other means to State and local agencies, special districts, and public or nonprofit agencies, institutions, and organizations, including the Estuary Partnership, for projects, activities, and studies identified on the annual priority list compiled under subsection (c).

children.heading: Maximum amount of grants; non-Federal share
children.label: 2 Maximum amount of grants; non-Federal share

children.heading: Maximum amount of grants
children.label: A Maximum amount of grants
children.text: Amounts provided to any entity under this section for a fiscal year shall not exceed an amount equal to 75 percent of the total cost of any projects, activities, and studies that are to be carried out using those amounts.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: B Non-Federal share
children.text: Not less than 25 percent of the cost of any project, activity, or study carried out using amounts provided under this section shall be provided from non-Federal sources.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 1 Administrative expenses
children.text: Of the amount made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year, the Director may not use more than 5 percent to pay administrative expenses incurred in carrying out this section.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 2 Prohibition
children.text: No amounts made available under this section may be used for the administration of a management conference under .section 1330 of this title
